prompt: Develop a JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling system for organizations that coordinate legal casework, appointments, and compliance tasks in air-gapped or low-connectivity environments. The Angular web UI provides distinct workspaces for Client, Lawyer, and Administrator roles, with role-based menus and in-context permission feedback when actions are restricted. Users sign in with locally verified username and password, with password rules requiring at least 12 characters including one number and one symbol, and an automatic lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes. Clients can place booking orders for consultations or case milestones, request reschedules, or cancel, while seeing clear policy messages such as “no-show after 10 minutes past the scheduled start counts as a violation” and “cancellations within 2 hours incur a credit penalty.” Lawyers manage availability and accept or decline bookings; administrators monitor utilization and operational health through dashboards that summarize availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput volume, and closed-loop efficiency for alerts and incidents, with filters by organization, date range, and role. Reports can be exported locally to CSV/Excel and saved to disk with an on-screen confirmation; scheduled report “subscriptions” generate queued, local in-app notifications in the header inbox at 8:00 AM daily without relying on email, SMS, or push services. After a task is completed, both client and lawyer are prompted to submit a two-way review with 1–5 star ratings across timeliness, professionalism, and communication plus up to 1,000 characters of comments; disputes can be appealed within 7 days and routed into an arbitration flow visible to admins.The backend uses Fastify to expose decoupled REST APIs consumed by the Angular frontend, with PostgreSQL for durable local persistence and auditability. Authentication is implemented with Spring-Security-like controls using JWT-style signed session tokens stored locally; cross-location login control is enforced by allowing one active session per user and expiring the prior session upon a new login, with configurable “admin exempt” rules. Authorization is tiered at both menu and API levels and further constrained by organization isolation, ensuring lawyers and clients only access their own matters while administrators can be scoped to a single organization or granted multi-tenant oversight. Booking concurrency is protected with a combination of database transactions and distributed locks implemented via PostgreSQL advisory locks, plus token-bucket rate limiting (for example, 20 booking writes per minute per user and 200 per minute per organization) to prevent overselling and to guarantee idempotency using client-generated request keys valid for 24 hours. A distributed job scheduling and queueing subsystem shards long-running tasks (such as incremental record crawling/import, report generation, and compliance checks) across multiple nodes, supports priorities, retries with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts, and idempotent execution guards; administrators can visually monitor job state, latency, and failure reasons. The rating and trust model maintains credit scores (0–100) with configurable rules such as -5 for late delivery, -10 for no-show, and +2 for five consecutive on-time completions, and every change is recorded with an explainable history visible to authorized staff. Integration is supported through offline-safe REST and optional local Webhook dispatch to on-prem endpoints, with configurable dictionaries and workflow steps stored in PostgreSQL; sensitive fields like notes and identifiers are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with a locally managed key, and logs mask personal data by default while retaining an immutable audit trail.
